text:
It’s the fastest-growing sport on the planet, and pickleball’s proponents are pushing for its inclusion in the Brisbane 2032 Games.

Organisers unveiled “Believe. Belong. Become. Brisbane 2032” as the motto for Australia’s third Olympics after consultation including 40 workshops. It might sound familiar.

Olympians and paralympians have revealed what the Games’ newly announced slogan means to them.

It took input from 6000 people, 37 stakeholder groups, 40 workshops and meetings, and came out of 1300 ideas – now the Brisbane Olympics and Paralympics have finally unveiled a “Games vision” in all its alliterative glory.

Protests over the Victoria Park stadium have inspired a stage show that takes an irreverent view of the development divide.

Six multimillion-dollar projects funded more than two years ago and set to wrap up by December have not started, although the state promised new delivery dates.

Engineers have warned infrastructure for the 2032 Olympic Games must be designed and delivered with the next half-century in mind.

For the 100-or-so people who assembled in Victoria Park to mark 150 years since its establishment on Wednesday morning, the mood was more resolute than celebratory.

As contenders for Brisbane Olympics inclusion are shortlisted, an American initiative is pushing to usurp traditional Australian codes.

Five-time Olympic gold swimmer Ian Thorpe has been added to the athletes and sports advisory group that provides input to the Brisbane 2032 board.
title: Topic | Brisbane 2032 Olympics | Brisbane Times
description: The latest Brisbane 2032 Olympics news, articles, data, and analysis from Brisbane Times
